reports.summary: SDA ("Semantic Digital Asset", Hodapp 2014 thesis / 2018 workshop paper) is a semiotic data model: every piece of information is an immutable atomic record, a 7-tuple (sigmatics, pragmatism, semantics, syntax, asset, fingerprint, editstamp) — identity, functional role, meaning, encoding, serialized payload, SHA-256 hash, creation timestamp. A store is an append-only event log with exactly two operations (emit/revoke); complex documents are flat collections of SDAs sharing a sigmatics (multi-faceted identity), recursively decomposable (document → paragraph → sentence → NER annotation), and queryable orthogonally on any of the four semiotic axes. On top of the core tuple sit conventional layers expressed as pragmatism values: identifiers (uid dedup keys), owl/sameAs (instance equivalence), skos/exactMatch (concept equivalence), dcterms/subject (classification), plus an outline hierarchy (has_parents/has_children) and SKOS ontology concepts stored as SDAs themselves. Two application thrusts drive storage requirements: (a) GoBD-compliant archiving (German tax-audit immutability/completeness/traceability/machine-readability, extended with a Merkle-tree audit layer, retention timers, legal hold, Z3 CSV export) and (b) "semiotic retrieval" — a three-layer search architecture (FTS5+SKOS synonyms, LLM reasoning agent over axes+outline, dual per-record embeddings "Triple Sigmatics"). Conceptually SDA is positioned against RDF triples, Datomic datoms, event sourcing, standoff annotation, and content-addressable storage; an RDF projection (sda: OWL vocabulary, hybrid "Approach C": SDA store primary, RDF as query/linking layer) is planned, not core. In datacrystal terms, the SDA tuple is a single fixed-schema record type plus categorical indexes — most of its query needs map directly onto datacrystal's planned primitives, but its storage contract demands append-only immutability, content hashing at commit, and a verifiable completeness/audit chain that datacrystal does not currently promise.

key_findings.title: The SDA is a fixed 7-tuple, not a graph or schema framework
key_findings.detail: CONCEPT.md:13-25 defines SDA = (sigmatics, pragmatism, semantics, syntax, asset, fingerprint, editstamp). Sigmatics = URI-compatible identity ('what entity?'); pragmatism = functional role ('sentence', 'annotation', 'topology', 'provenance', 'identifiers', 'owl', 'skos', 'dcterms'); semantics = meaning within that role ('original', 'translation', 'entity-reference', 'sameAs', 'subject'); syntax = encoding (MIME types, language codes like 'en'/'de', DSL ids like 'topology:item'); asset = serialized payload (text/DSL/blob); fingerprint = SHA-256 of asset computed at emit; editstamp = temporal ordering. All dimension values are open strings — 'schema-free' is listed as an explicit limitation (CONCEPT.md:368).

key_findings.title: Invariants and lifecycle: append-only event sourcing with only emit and revoke
key_findings.detail: Core principles (CONCEPT.md:153-160): flat over nested, immutability (SDAs never modified — changes create new assets), event sourcing (emit = add, revoke = remove-marker that is itself a new record), orthogonal querying, multi-faceted elements, recursive decomposition. Revoke grows the log, never shrinks it (gobd-ledger-research.md:207). 'Current state' is a projection (DocElem grouping by sigmatics, CONCEPT.md:277-281). The only true deletion is retention-expiry deletion with documented Merkle-tree rebuild (gobd-ledger-research.md:224-228). Explicitly modeled on Datomic datoms (entity≈sigmatics, attribute≈pragmatism+semantics, transaction≈editstamp, added?≈emit/revoke; CONCEPT.md:252-270).

key_findings.title: Multi-faceted identity is the central design move
key_findings.detail: One sigmatics, many SDAs: a ZUGFeRD invoice is archived as 4 SDAs sharing sigmatics 'inv-2026-042' — original CII-XML, PDF/A visual, JSON classification annotation, JSON booking entry (gobd-ledger-research.md:253-258). Recursive decomposition example CONCEPT.md:135-145: HTML doc → paragraphs → sentences → FOODON entity-reference, all flat records linked by hierarchical sigmatics or topology SDAs. Multiple annotations on the same span are disambiguated by fingerprint (CONCEPT.md:307-314). This is standoff annotation generalized to all data.

key_findings.title: Terminology drift and a published erratum exist
key_findings.detail: CONCEPT.md uses 'pragmatism'; RELATIONS.md consistently uses 'pragmatics' as the field name (e.g. RELATIONS.md:34, 'pragmatics: identifiers'); semiotic-retrieval-research.md:76 uses SQL column 'pragmatism'. CONCEPT.md:43 and ref [2] note the 2018 published paper inadvertently swapped the semantics and pragmatism columns. Any datacrystal-level adoption must pick canonical field names; the concept itself treats them as synonyms.

key_findings.title: The relation model: 4 vocabulary layers + instances-vs-concepts split (RELATIONS.md)
key_findings.detail: Two entity kinds: instances (real invoices/emails, produced by SWARM pipelines) and concepts (SKOS vocabulary terms from TTL imports or Business Map boards). Four relation layers, each a distinct pragmatics value: (1) identifiers — properties uid (required, scoped URI 'sig://{vendor_slug}/{vendor_id}', the dedup key), primaryId, alsoKnownAs (borrowed from W3C DID); (2) owl/sameAs — bidirectional, idempotent instance-equivalence links written only by the automated dedup service when uids match; (3) skos/exactMatch — concept equivalence created by terminology import; (4) dcterms/subject — instance→concept classification written by SWARM and a retroactive linker. Claimed novelty: identity as a queryable pragmatics layer ('WHERE pragmatics = identifiers' without schema knowledge, RELATIONS.md:204-206). Relations are SDAs whose asset is the target URI — i.e. typed edges stored as ordinary records.

key_findings.title: Ontology and hierarchy live INSIDE the store as ordinary SDAs
key_findings.detail: SKOS concepts are stored as SDA rows (semiotic-retrieval-research.md:108-111): sigmatic 7KxmP2 with pragmatism=skos and semantics=label/definition/broader/synonym. Document structure is an 'outline hierarchy' via has_parents/has_children relations on SDA groups (semiotic-retrieval-research.md:89-99). So tree (PageIndex-style), entity graph (GraphRAG-style), and ontology hypergraph (OG-RAG-style) are all encodings within the one flat record model — the thesis of the planned 'semiotic retrieval' paper is that this unification is the contribution.

key_findings.title: GoBD demands: the storage-layer contract SDA must guarantee
key_findings.detail: gobd-ledger-research.md maps SDA to the four GoBD principles: Unveränderbarkeit (append-only + fingerprint), Vollständigkeit (sigmatics grouping + NEW Merkle layer — per-document hashes are insufficient, collection-level completeness proof is the identified gap, line 62), Nachvollziehbarkeit (editstamp + provenance/topology SDAs), Maschinelle Auswertbarkeit (syntax field + Z3 export: flat CSV + index.xml + DTD per Beschreibungsstandard v1.6). Architecture (§5): SQLite SDA store + Merkle audit layer (leaf = SDA fingerprint, root recomputed per emit, root-hash chain, O(log n) proofs, checkpoints optionally signed via RFC 3161/eIDAS timestamps) + optional WORM blob backend. Retention: timers derived from pragmatism (invoice→8y, annual-statement→10y, correspondence→6y per BEG IV), legal-hold flag, expiry deletion requires audited tree rebuild. IDW PS 880 certification is a medium-term goal.

key_findings.title: Retrieval semantics: orthogonal 4-axis queries + three-layer search architecture
key_findings.detail: Queries are conjunctive equality filters over categorical axes plus sigmatics lookup (semiotic-retrieval-research.md:74-83, literal SQL: WHERE pragmatism='object' AND semantics='classification' AND syntax='json'; WHERE sigmatics='X' ORDER BY pragmatism, semantics). Layered search (§3.4): Layer 1 symbolic = FTS5 + SKOS synonym expansion; Layer 2 reasoning = LLM agent with three tools (SPARQL over in-memory RDFLib concept graph, outline navigator over has_parents/has_children, FTS5); Layer 3 embedding = 'Triple Sigmatics'. Evaluation plan targets LongMemEval (>86%) and FinanceBench (vs PageIndex 98.7%) with ablations vs naive RAG/GraphRAG/PageIndex.

key_findings.title: Proposed model extensions: authority field and Triple Sigmatics
key_findings.detail: OUTLOOK.md Direction A: optional 8th tuple element 'authority' — an integer (0–1000); when multiple SDAs share sigmatics+pragmatism+semantics, highest authority is the effective value at query time, lower ones persist but are shadowed (CRDT-friendly conflict resolution + GoBD-compatible audit: 'LLM generated X (200) → human corrected to Y (700)'). authority=0 marks volatile, GC-able data exempt from retention. The org-specific level mapping is explicitly 'configuration, not part of the SDA model' (OUTLOOK.md:245). Open question whether authority is core tuple or annotation SDA. Direction B: sigmatics becomes (id, embedding_semantic, embedding_asset) — two vectors per record (~12KB at 1536 dims), embedding_asset recomputed on new content versions, old vectors retained with model-version tags for temporal similarity queries. CRDT-as-document-model (ops-as-SDAs with editstamp causal ordering) is also sketched but unprototyped.

key_findings.title: RDF is a projection, not the substrate — 'Approach C hybrid' is the stated recommendation
key_findings.detail: gobd-ledger-research.md:276-283 evaluates three options and recommends C: SDA store as primary storage, RDF graph (via URI sigmatics, P2P-O ontology for invoices) as query/linking layer, SPARQL without full data transformation. OUTLOOK.md drafts a minimal sda: RDFS/OWL-Lite vocabulary (sda:SDA class + 7 datatype properties) deliberately without inference requirements. CONCEPT.md:364-369 lists no-native-inference, no recursive graph traversal ('flat queries — topology must be processed'), and schema-freedom as accepted limitations.

key_findings.title: Timeless concept vs implementation detail
key_findings.detail: Timeless (the publishable model): the 7-tuple and semiotic axis definitions; immutability + emit/revoke; multi-faceted sigmatics identity; recursive decomposition; orthogonal querying; URI-compatible identity; instances-vs-concepts distinction; relation layers reusing standard vocabularies (owl/skos/dcterms/DID terms); fingerprint+editstamp as first-class integrity/temporality. Implementation detail / contingent: SQLite backing, '.sdaout' corpus format (CONCEPT.md:279), FTS5, RDFLib in-memory graph, the 'sig://{vendor}/{id}' uid pattern, SWARM pipeline ownership rules, Merkle tree as SQLite adjacency list, Z3/Beschreibungsstandard export, authority mapping tables, retention period numbers, spike numbers (#228, #286). Unsettled model questions: authority as 8th field, pragmatism-vs-pragmatics naming, Triple Sigmatics, CRDT extension. The implementation dir (/Users/sh/tetrahedron/projects/semantic-studio/src/semantic_studio/sda_store/: core.py, blob.py, tenant.py, plugins/, realm_import.py) confirms multi-tenancy and blob handling are real requirements though barely mentioned in the concept docs.

key_findings.title: Strategic context: SDA is an academic/publishable concept with a closing window
key_findings.detail: OUTLOOK.md defines a 5-paper pipeline: Paper 1 formal model (foundation, 'HIGH priority'), Paper 2 GoBD ledger (with co-author Björn Müller, audit-domain credibility), Paper 3 SDA↔RDF mapping, Paper R semiotic retrieval ('HIGH — competitive window closing', position paper targeted at SEMANTiCS 2026, deadline ~May 2026 — i.e. essentially now relative to 2026-06-10), Paper 4 LLM extraction. This matters for the build-on vs build-in question: the SDA model's identity as a citable, formally defined concept argues for keeping it a named layer with its own invariants rather than dissolving it into a general-purpose database's feature set.

reports.datacrystal_relevance: GENERIC persistence primitives (storage-layer concerns SDA needs, datacrystal-shaped): (1) The SDA tuple is ONE fixed-schema record type — in datacrystal it is a single msgspec-record entity (7 fields: 4 short strings, 1 bytes/str payload, 1 hash, 1 int timestamp), not a new storage paradigm; sigmatics maps onto OID/secondary-unique-key lookup. (2) Orthogonal querying over pragmatism/semantics/syntax is filtering on low-cardinality categorical strings — a near-perfect fit for pyroaring bitmap indexes + the Condition AST; 'all facets of sigmatics X ordered by axes' is a bitmap-AND plus sort. (3) Layer-1 symbolic search = SQLite FTS5 sidecar (already planned); Layer-3 Triple Sigmatics = usearch vector sidecar, but requires TWO vectors per record plus model-version-tagged historical vectors — a multi-vector-sidecar generalization of datacrystal's current design, fed naturally by the idempotent commit-delta/watermark pipeline (embedding_asset recomputed exactly when a new SDA version for a sigmatics lands in a commit delta). (4) owl:sameAs / dcterms:subject / has_parents-has_children edges map onto the v1 reverse-reference index + traversal API (SDA stores edges as rows with URI assets — datacrystal could store them as typed references and get reverse traversal for free, fixing SDA's admitted 'flat queries, no recursive traversal' limitation). (5) The RDF 'Approach C hybrid' (store primary, RDF projection for SPARQL) is exactly datacrystal's planned RDF/triples extension package — SDA's sda: vocabulary is a ready-made projection target. (6) Z3 CSV export, pivot tables, and 'Maschinelle Auswertbarkeit' map onto Arrow columnar mirrors (DuckDB/polars). (7) Datomic-style append-only semantics: SDA's emit/revoke + editstamp resembles datacrystal's immutable watermark snapshots, but SDA demands it as the WRITE model, not just the read model — datacrystal's tri-state dirty tracking / mutable entities would need an 'append-only collection' mode where update is forbidden and revoke is a record. (8) Fingerprint-at-commit and a Merkle/hash-chained commit log (root hash per commit, O(log n) inclusion proofs, completeness proof between watermarks) is a genuinely generic primitive candidate — it slots onto the watermark/commit-delta pipeline and would differentiate datacrystal, but is NOT currently in its design. (9) tenant.py in the existing sda_store hints at multi-tenancy; datacrystal's embedded local-first persona handles this as DB-per-tenant at the application layer. DOMAIN/application semantics (belong ON TOP of datacrystal, in an SDA package): the semiotic INTERPRETATION of the four axes and their vocabularies; the identifiers/owl/skos/dcterms layer conventions and ownership rules (dedup service writes sameAs, importers write exactMatch); the dedup service, terminology importer, retroactive linker, SWARM extraction pipelines; the LLM reasoning search agent; authority-level mapping tables (explicitly 'configuration, not model'); GoBD specifics — retention-period policy per pragmatism, legal hold, Z3/Beschreibungsstandard export, Verfahrensdokumentation templates, IDW PS 880; P2P-O invoice ontology. BORDERLINE (could go either way): query-time authority shadowing (max-wins projection — generic 'effective view over event log' vs app logic); revoke-aware current-state projection (generic event-sourcing read-model machinery); recursive-decomposition lifecycle (app workflow, but benefits from generic lazy loading via Lazy[T] for unparsed assets/blobs). Net shape: SDA-as-concept is an application-level data model + vocabulary discipline that needs from its storage layer exactly four things datacrystal doesn't yet promise — enforced append-only collections, content fingerprints at commit, a verifiable hash-chain/Merkle audit primitive, and multi-vector sidecars — while everything else SDA queries on is already on datacrystal's roadmap (bitmap indexes, FTS5/usearch sidecars, reverse references, Arrow mirrors, RDF extension, snapshots).
sources: /Users/sh/tetrahedron/projects/semantic-studio/src/semantic_studio/sda_store/ (directory listing only: core.py, blob.py, tenant.py, db_setup.py, realm_import.py, plugins/)

reports.summary: SDA store is an append-only EAV/quad store on SQLite (via SQLModel/SQLAlchemy async + aiosqlite) where every fact is one row in a single table `sda_store`: (sigmatic, pragmatics, semantics, syntax, asset, ingested_at). `sigmatic` is a URI identity (36-bit-random base58 base, dotted suffixes for hierarchy), `pragmatics` is a layer/namespace ('outline', 'business-map', 'identifiers', 'de.semanticworks.invoice'), `semantics` is the attribute name, `syntax` is a type tag ('txt','uri','json','outline','blob_ref','datetime'), and `asset` is the value, always normalized to a JSON-list string with cardinality (0-n/1-1/0-1/1-n) validated only in Python. Updates never mutate: `update_sda` simply inserts a new row and "latest version" is recomputed at read time via GROUP BY/MAX(ingested_at) self-joins. A plugin system injects SQL views; the deduplication plugin builds owl:sameAs/alsoKnownAs clusters with a recursive CTE and exposes "virtual SDAs" (redirects, cluster TOCs) through a `sda_union` view that ALL reads go through. Multi-tenancy is DB-per-tenant (`data/{org_id}/studio.db`) via a TenantRegistry with per-tenant asyncio locks; a co-located `blob_store` table holds binaries content-addressed by SHA-256, linked back as `syntax='blob_ref'` SDAs. Objects (outlines, boards) are reconstructed in Python by grouping rows per sigmatic and recursively following `has_children:uri` JSON arrays; a separate `search_index` + FTS5 table is rebuilt from scratch by walking every outline tree. The slowness is structural: every exact-URI read recomputes the entire dedup connected-components graph, every "latest" read GROUP-BYs over an ever-growing version history including the full asset text, and reads of one logical object fan out into N+1 per-node queries with per-row json.loads + Pydantic model construction.

key_findings.title: Schema: single EAV table + blob store + 5 views
key_findings.detail: core.py:301-340 defines `sda_store(id PK, sigmatic TEXT, pragmatics TEXT, semantics TEXT, syntax TEXT, asset TEXT, ingested_at DATETIME)` plus a `volatile INTEGER DEFAULT 0` column added by the dedup plugin (views.py:153-156). Indexes: idx_sda_sigmatic, idx_sda_sigmatic_pragmatics_semantics, idx_sda_ingested_at (core.py:520-538). blob.py:77-116 creates `blob_store(id, sigmatic, mime_type, filename, data BLOB, created_at, content_hash)` with indexes on sigmatic and content_hash. The dedup plugin adds views v_alt_pairs, v_clusters, v_object_toc, v_redirects, sda_union (views.py). Asset values are ALWAYS JSON-list strings (normalize_asset, core.py:184-213); cardinality (Cardinality enum core.py:172-181) is enforced only by an optional Python validator, never by the DB.

key_findings.title: Operations API: CRUD-by-append, prefix queries, no real relation traversal
key_findings.detail: SDAService (core.py:417+) offers create_sda (insert), update_sda == create_sda (core.py:776-791, versions accumulate forever, only unpublish_outline at core.py:1021 ever deletes), get_sdas_by_uri / get_sdas_by_uris_batch (IN-clause batch, core.py:601-664), get_sdas_by_uri_prefix (LIKE 'prefix%'), get_latest_by_sigmatic, get_version_history, raw execute_query. Hierarchy = URI string convention: get_hierarchy_descendants is a LIKE 'base.%' query (core.py:793-797); siblings are filtered in Python by string depth (core.py:799-818). Relations are NOT first-class: create_relation (core.py:851-864) writes an SDA with pragmatics='relation' and asset string 'from_rel_to'; get_related_sdas (core.py:831-849) does Python substring containment `sigmatic_uri in sda.asset` — reverse-reference lookup ('who points at me') has no index and degrades to scans. Graph edges in practice are `has_children:uri` / `has_parents:uri` JSON arrays inside asset.

key_findings.title: Plugin architecture: SQL-view injection + named capability hooks
key_findings.detail: plugins/base.py defines SDAPlugin ABC with hooks: on_start(service), provide_views() -> list of CREATE VIEW DDL, extend_queries(query_context) (defined but unused anywhere), refresh_views, get_status, and dedup-specific capability methods get_object_versions / get_canonical_sigmatic / get_cluster_members that the core service dispatches to by plugin name (core.py:880-897 raises NotImplementedError without the plugin). SDAPluginRegistry is a class-level global list with priority ordering (base.py:110-154); plugins auto-register at import time (__init__.py:39). So the only real extension mechanism that ships is: a plugin can splice SQL views into the read path — which the dedup plugin uses to redefine what ALL reads see.

key_findings.title: Multi-tenancy: DB-per-tenant with lazy registry
key_findings.detail: tenant.py: TenantRegistry maps org_id -> SDAService, each tenant gets `data/{org_id}/studio.db` (tenant.py:121-123) with its own AsyncEngine; default tenant '_default'. get_or_create (tenant.py:75-110) lazily mkdirs, creates engine, runs create_tables (idempotent schema + plugin view bootstrap) under a per-tenant asyncio.Lock with double-checked caching. get() is sync cache lookup that raises if uninitialised. BlobService is co-located on the same engine per tenant (blob.py:31-47). db_setup.setup_all_tenants iterates data/*/studio.db dirs. Clean, simple isolation model — no cross-tenant queries exist or are possible.

key_findings.title: Slowness root cause #1: every read recomputes dedup clusters via recursive CTE
key_findings.detail: core.py:455-464 `_get_read_table` routes ALL read queries through the `sda_union` view. sda_union (views.py:140-150) UNION-ALLs sda_store with v_object_toc and v_redirects, both built on v_clusters (views.py:21-66) — a WITH RECURSIVE flood-fill connected-components computation over v_alt_pairs, which itself runs json_each() over every `pragmatics='identifiers' AND semantics='alsoKnownAs'` asset (views.py:10-18). SQLite recomputes this entire graph clustering on EVERY query, even `get_sdas_by_uri('X')` for one URI — predicate pushdown cannot shortcut a recursive CTE or the json_group_array aggregations in v_object_toc. Cost grows superlinearly with the alsoKnownAs graph and linearly with table size for every single read. This is the dominant 'slow when growing' mechanism.

key_findings.title: Slowness root cause #2: latest-version dedup is a per-read GROUP BY over full history including the asset text
key_findings.detail: Because updates append rows forever, 'latest' is computed at read time by a self-join: GROUP BY sigmatic, pragmatics, semantics, syntax, asset with MAX(ingested_at), joined back on all five columns including equality on the full JSON `asset` text (core.py:467-484, 494-511, 631-646). Grouping/joining on an unbounded TEXT column is unindexable through the sda_union view and scans all versions of all matching rows. Every edit to an entity makes every future read of it slower; nothing ever compacts old versions. There is no per-version-tuple index, and the GROUP BY happens over the view, so the sda_store indexes (core.py:520-538) only help the inner WHERE.

key_findings.title: Slowness root cause #3: N+1 object reconstruction in Python
key_findings.detail: A logical object is K rows; reading it means fetching rows then json.loads per row (get_asset_values core.py:216-231) plus SDA(**row) Pydantic construction per row with a model_fields filter dict-comp (core.py:683-685 and 5 more copies). Tree builds are recursive one-query-per-node: render_processor/outline_tree.py:16-91 (build_outline_tree calls get_sdas_by_uri per child); the batch variant (outline_tree.py:94-197, one IN-query per BFS level) exists but many hot paths still loop: search/index.py:384 and :619 (per child_uri / per parent in breadcrumb walk), search/article_detail.py:61+90 (parent-chain walk, one query per ancestor), terminology/board_enrichment.py:95 (per board element), dedup/service.py:472. blob.py:275-283 _ensure_blob_sda fetches all SDAs for a sigmatic and scans them in Python just to check existence.

key_findings.title: Slowness root cause #4: rebuild-the-world maintenance jobs
key_findings.detail: db_setup.setup_database (db_setup.py:270-284) runs batch dedup AND a full search-index rebuild on every setup. SearchIndexService.rebuild (search/index.py:136-198) discovers all roots, walks every outline tree (re-querying SDA store throughout), calls get_max_ingested_at_by_sigmatic — a full-table GROUP BY materialized into a Python dict for ALL sigmatics (core.py:900-922) — then DELETEs the whole search_index and re-inserts row-by-row via ORM session.add, then issues FTS5 'rebuild' which re-reads everything (index.py:117-130). import_json_data (core.py:931-975) creates one AsyncSession + one COMMIT per attribute row — per-row fsync makes bulk import O(rows) transactions. discover_root_outlines (outline_tree.py:307-341) selects ALL pragmatics='outline' rows and computes roots in Python; get_published_outlines (core.py:1047-1066) filters on pragmatics+semantics which no index leads with, scanning sda_union (including the recursive CTE).

key_findings.title: Versioning & dedup semantics worth keeping
key_findings.detail: Conceptually strong pieces: (a) append-only versioning with ingested_at and a version-tuple identity — time travel for free; (b) content-addressed blob store (SHA-256, idempotent upsert, hash-verified reads, blob_ref SDAs placing binaries inside the semantic model, blob.py:118-218); (c) entity dedup as data, not destruction: identifiers/uid -> owl:sameAs links stored as ordinary SDAs, canonical election by oldest-wins with manual override (dedup/service.py docstring lines 1-52), and 'virtual SDAs' (redirects + cluster TOC) that make merged identities transparently readable at original URIs; (d) the batch query API get_sdas_by_uris_batch explicitly built to kill N+1 (core.py:601-664); (e) idempotent, re-runnable migrations (db_setup.py:32-178).

key_findings.title: The four-layer semantic model is the actual invention
key_findings.detail: The durable idea is the addressing scheme: sigmatic (identity URI) x pragmatics (layer/context namespace) x semantics (attribute) x syntax (type tag) -> asset (value list with cardinality). It lets multiple producers (REALM import, SWARM sync, SKOS terminology enrichment, blob ingest, manual edits) write into different layers of the SAME identity without coordinating schemas — realm_import.py merges 'outline' + 'business-map' layers per sigmatic (realm_import.py:91-132); board_enrichment adds a SKOS layer post-hoc. The 'semantics:syntax' key convention in import JSON (core.py:954-962, e.g. 'has_children:uri', 'title:txt') is a compact serialization of typed attributes. This layered-namespace-on-one-identity model is genuinely generic; everything else (outline trees, boards, publish flags, invoice heuristics in blob.py:220-244) is application vocabulary.

reports.datacrystal_relevance: GENERIC persistence primitives (belong in/under datacrystal): identity URIs (sigmatic ≈ datacrystal OID registry — base58 random base + hierarchical child suffixes maps to OIDs + owned sub-entities); append-only versioning + latest-view (maps to datacrystal's immutable watermark snapshots — 'latest' should be a maintained state, not a per-read GROUP BY); content-addressed blob store (a natural datacrystal sidecar alongside FTS/vector); DB-per-tenant registry (maps to one datacrystal store per directory; the TenantRegistry pattern survives as app code opening multiple stores); batch fetch API (subsumed by datacrystal entity loads + Lazy[T] with reverse-reference index); search_index/FTS5 rebuild pipeline (exactly what datacrystal's idempotent commit-delta/watermark pipeline replaces — incremental instead of delete-all+rebuild); the unused extend_queries hook is what Condition AST queries actually deliver. DOMAIN/application semantics (stay on top): pragmatics layer vocabulary ('outline', 'business-map', 'PBI', invoice mime-type heuristics blob.py:220-244), REALM board validation/slugs, SKOS enrichment, publish/unpublish, outline tree rendering. MAPPING PER SDA ELEMENT: the (sigmatic, pragmatics, semantics, syntax, asset) quad is isomorphic to datacrystal's planned RDF/triples extension (sigmatic=subject, pragmatics+semantics=predicate-with-namespace, syntax=datatype tag, asset=object list) — SDA-as-schema-free-layers fits the RDF extension package, while stable layers (outline nodes, boards) should become typed slots-dataclass entities with cardinality expressed as field types (1-1 → T, 0-1 → T|None, 0-n → list[T]), has_children:uri → list[Lazy[Node]] resolved via the v1 reverse-reference index (killing the Python substring scans in core.py:845-849 and the parent-chain N+1 walks); dedup clusters → application logic over bitmap indexes + reverse-reference index, with connected components maintained incrementally at write time (union-find on commit) instead of the per-read recursive CTE; v_redirects/v_object_toc 'virtual SDAs' → derived entities refreshed by the watermark pipeline; get_max_ingested_at_by_sigmatic / table_export → Arrow columnar mirrors (DuckDB/polars) instead of full-table GROUP BY into Python dicts; search_index_fts → datacrystal's FTS5 sidecar fed by commit deltas. VERDICT IMPLICATION FROM EVIDENCE: every identified slowness cause (per-read recursive CTE, per-read version GROUP BY, N+1 reconstruction, rebuild-the-world indexing, per-row commits) is a symptom of building persistence machinery (versioning, dedup views, latest-resolution, search feeding) inside application SQL — these are precisely the primitives datacrystal already plans (snapshots, indexes, watermark pipeline, reverse refs). The SDA four-layer addressing model is a data-modeling vocabulary, not a storage engine: it fits cleanly as an extension package (like the planned RDF extension, possibly the same one) plus typed entities for hot paths, NOT as a change to datacrystal's core concept.

reports.summary: SDA (Semantic Digital Asset) is Sven's semiotic data model from his 2014 thesis / 2018 paper: every fact is an immutable 5-tuple (sigmatic=URI identity, pragmatics=functional layer, semantics=attribute, syntax=format, asset=JSON-list payload) plus ingested_at — essentially a Datomic-style append-only EAV/datom store where the attribute is split into two orthogonal dimensions. The concept doc explicitly positions it against Datomic, RDF, event sourcing, and standoff annotation. The implementation (sda_store, ~2600 LOC, SQLModel/aiosqlite, one SQLite table per tenant) backs "semantic-studio", a FastAPI+NiceGUI app whose primary domain is outline-centric documents: hierarchical outlines (has_children/has_parents URI lists), a "published" datetime SDA as head-document marker, PBI status, business-map boards, SWARM-agent-pushed invoices, SKOS terminology concepts, and entity deduplication via identifiers/uid → owl:sameAs. Reads are dominated by exact-sigmatic latest-version lookups, BFS tree walks, pragmatics/semantics raw-SQL scans, and a denormalized search_index table with external-content FTS5; writes are bursty agent pushes and editor edits, each followed by a FULL search-index rebuild. The reported growth slowness has clear structural causes: every read routes through an sda_union view that recomputes a recursive-CTE connected-components dedup clustering, latest-version resolution self-joins on the full asset text, and O(corpus) index rebuilds per write batch. Roughly 70% of sda_store is generic persistence machinery (versioned facts, identity, traversal, indexing, FTS/vector sidecars, multi-tenancy) that maps almost one-to-one onto datacrystal's planned features; the semiotic vocabulary, outline/SKOS/dedup policies, and render/search-ranking logic are application semantics.

key_findings.title: SDA model = immutable semiotic 5-tuple, consciously Datomic/RDF-adjacent
key_findings.detail: docs/concepts/SDA/CONCEPT.md defines SDA=(sigmatics, pragmatism, semantics, syntax, asset, fingerprint, editstamp); core principles: flat over nested, immutability, emit/revoke event sourcing, orthogonal querying, recursive decomposition of assets into finer SDAs. Explicit comparison table to Datomic datoms (entity≈sigmatics, attribute≈pragmatics+semantics, transaction≈editstamp). Known limitations listed: no inference, no recursive graph traversal ('topology must be processed'), schema-free = no validation. OUTLOOK.md plans 4+ academic papers (formal model, GoBD ledger, SDA-meets-RDF, semiotic retrieval, CRDT direction) — SDA is a research/publication agenda, not just an implementation detail.

key_findings.title: Implementation: single append-only SQLite table, latest-wins resolved at read time
key_findings.detail: sda_store/core.py:301-340: table sda_store(id, sigmatic, pragmatics, semantics, syntax, asset TEXT, ingested_at) with 3 indexes (sigmatic; sigmatic+pragmatics+semantics; ingested_at). update_sda just calls create_sda (core.py:776). All assets normalized to JSON lists (normalize_asset, core.py:184) with cardinality enum 0-n/1-1/0-1/1-n. 'Latest version' is computed per read via GROUP BY (sigmatic,pragmatics,semantics,syntax,asset) + MAX(ingested_at) self-join including the full asset column (core.py:467-511) — there is no version chain per attribute, only exact-tuple dedup, so edits accumulate as parallel facts.

key_findings.title: Root cause candidates for 'slow when growing': sda_union recursive CTE on EVERY read
key_findings.detail: sda_store/plugins/deduplication/views.py: all SDAService reads go through view sda_union = sda_store UNION ALL v_object_toc UNION ALL v_redirects (core.py:455-464 _get_read_table). v_clusters is a RECURSIVE flood-fill connected-components CTE over all identifiers/alsoKnownAs pairs (json_each over assets), re-evaluated per query with no materialization. Combined with the 5-column self-join for latest-only and LIKE prefix scans, read cost grows superlinearly with row count. A 'volatile' column exists 'for future materialization' but is unused.

key_findings.title: Write path: bursty agent pushes with read-back idempotency, then O(corpus) reindex
key_findings.detail: swarm_integration/business_logic.py push_objects (lines ~640-845): each SWARM object becomes 1 SDA per data field + auto outline title/published + comment/review_reason + blob, after fetching ALL existing SDAs for the sigmatic and comparing (pragmatics,semantics,syntax,normalized_asset) tuples in Python. Hierarchy building creates inbox→sync-batch→object nodes with has_parents/has_children SDAs. post_push_maintenance then runs per-sigmatic dedup AND SearchIndexService.rebuild() — a FULL delete-all+rewalk of every outline tree, board, and SKOS concept (search/index.py:136-198) plus FTS5 rebuild. Same full rebuild after terminology import, admin import, and on startup if empty (main.py:86).

key_findings.title: Read/query shapes are narrow and index-friendly
key_findings.detail: Dominant patterns: (1) exact-sigmatic fetch of all latest SDAs (get_sdas_by_uri), (2) batch IN-clause variant added to fix N+1 (get_sdas_by_uris_batch, core.py:601), (3) raw SQL scans WHERE pragmatics='X' [AND semantics='Y'] (discover_root_outlines, board/terminology collection, synonym expansion via LOWER(asset) LIKE), (4) BFS tree build: level-by-level batch queries O(depth) (render_processor/outline_tree.py:94-197), (5) published-outline listing ORDER BY asset DESC. The hierarchical dot-suffix URI scheme (parent.1.2) with LIKE-prefix queries exists in core.py but the real hierarchy is has_children/has_parents URI-list assets — the prefix machinery is largely vestigial.

key_findings.title: Search: denormalized search_index table + external-content FTS5 + SKOS synonym expansion
key_findings.detail: search/index.py: one row per outline node (title, description, topics_flat=whole-subtree text, breadcrumbs_json, is_published, content_type ∈ {'', business-map, board-element, terminology-concept}, updated_at bubbled from descendants). FTS5 external-content table with BM25 rank, prefix tokens (term*), hyphen sanitization, two-pass query (title/description then topics_flat for concepts). Query-time synonym→canonical-label expansion via SQL LIKE on skos/synonym assets, OR-ed label groups (deliberately one-directional after Sprint-32 over-expansion incident). Incremental refresh_for_sigmatic exists but full rebuild() is what's actually called after writes.

key_findings.title: Terminology integration: SKOS concepts ARE SDAs; binding via 4 relation layers
key_findings.detail: specs/terminology-integration.md (approved design): concepts stored under pragmatics='skos' with semantics ∈ {label, definition, broader, narrower, related, synonym, exactMatch, source_uri} — 'no new tables', deliberately inheriting multi-tenancy/versioning/search/rendering. docs/concepts/SDA/RELATIONS.md formalizes 4 layers: identifiers (uid/primaryId/alsoKnownAs, W3C-DID-inspired identity properties), owl/sameAs (instance equivalence created by DedupService from shared uid, bidirectional+idempotent), skos/exactMatch (concept↔board-element equivalence), dcterms/subject (instance→concept classification by SWARM + retroactive linker). Terminologies: EN16931/ZUGFeRD invoice fields, SWARM-extracted entities, Business-Map-derived concepts; TTL import as bootstrap. Live dev DB: 682 rows — skos 286, object 256, semantic-topic 81, comment 58.

key_findings.title: Planned 3-layer search puts SPARQL/RDF and vectors on the roadmap
key_findings.detail: terminology-integration.md Waves: 1 FTS5+SKOS (done), 2 synonym expansion/board extraction/dedup (done), 3 reasoning agent with three tools — SPARQL over RDFLib in-memory graph, outline navigator over has_parents/has_children, FTS5 — explicitly arguing 'the SDA model is already a structured retrieval index... richer signal than vectors' (citing PageIndex 98.7% FinanceBench), 4 Ollama+sqlite-vec embeddings. rdflib currently appears only in terminology/business_logic.py (TTL parsing). This is precisely datacrystal's FTS5 sidecar + RDF extension + usearch vector sidecar + traversal API trio.

key_findings.title: Outline is the primary concept; 'published' SDA is the head-document marker
key_findings.detail: specs/outline-sda-concept.txt: outline = successor of 'semantic topic', the overarching baseline concept; title/description/has_children (syntax=uri for refs, syntax=outline for volatile inline children)/has_parents (informational only — has_children is authoritative); PBI status enum renders as checkboxes; assets always lists with cardinality constraints; (id, outline, published, datetime, ts) marks a head document — presence = visible in overview, value = sort key, deletion = unpublish (the ONLY hard DELETE in the service besides migrations, core.py:1021). UI consumers: document overview newest-first, Vue outline editor (flat rows + level ints, annotation_type/needs_review enrichment with parent roll-up counts), article detail, /concepts read-only page, admin debug panel, table/pivot export.

key_findings.title: Multi-tenancy and blobs are infrastructure already shaped like datacrystal's persona
key_findings.detail: sda_store/tenant.py: DB-per-tenant SQLite at data/{org_id}/studio.db, lazily created via per-tenant asyncio.Lock, cached SDAService per org; default tenant '_default' for local dev. sda_store/blob.py: co-located blob_store table with sha256 content_hash dedup (#270), blobs referenced from SDAs via syntax='blob_ref'; comment says 'interim — will be replaced with S3-compatible store later'. Everything is asyncio (FastAPI lifespan singletons + module-level service registry) — note datacrystal's owner-thread confinement contract would need an asyncio-facing story (single-event-loop ownership) for this consumer.

key_findings.title: Functional requirements SDA actually serves today (the contract any successor must keep)
key_findings.detail: (1) schema-free multi-layer facts about a URI-identified entity, appendable by independent producers (SWARM, importer, editor, dedup service) without coordination; (2) latest-wins read with full history retained (audit/GoBD ambitions, gobd-ledger-research.md); (3) outline tree traversal both directions + root discovery; (4) published listing; (5) FTS search with synonym expansion and breadcrumb-renderable results; (6) entity dedup with canonical election (oldest wins) and redirect resolution; (7) idempotent re-import/re-push; (8) per-tenant isolation; (9) blob attachment with hash dedup; (10) tabular export of layer attributes (table_export flattens SDAs to pivot rows); (11) cross-vocabulary concept linking. Write volume is small (hundreds-to-thousands of rows per tenant today); the pain is read amplification and rebuild cost, not write throughput.

reports.datacrystal_relevance: GENERIC persistence primitives currently hand-rolled inside sda_store (candidates to be ABSORBED by datacrystal): (1) append-only versioned fact storage with latest-wins reads and retained history → datacrystal records + immutable watermark snapshots (SDA's ingested_at ≈ watermark; 'time-travel by tuple' falls out for free); (2) sigmatic URI identity → OID registry (sigmatic strings as aliases on OIDs); (3) pragmatics/semantics/syntax equality filters (all current raw-SQL scans) → pyroaring bitmap indexes + Condition AST — this directly replaces the worst LIKE/scan queries; (4) has_children/has_parents traversal, root discovery, sibling lookup → v1 reverse-reference index + traversal API (kills the BFS batch-query walker and _find_root_sigmatic loop); (5) search_index + FTS5 full rebuilds → FTS5 sidecar fed by the idempotent commit-delta/watermark pipeline — this is the single biggest fix for the reported slowness, converting O(corpus)-per-write into O(delta); (6) planned Wave-4 sqlite-vec embeddings → usearch vector sidecar; (7) planned Wave-3 SPARQL/RDFLib reasoning tool → the RDF/triples extension package (an SDA row (sig, prag, sem, syn, asset) maps to a quad: subject=sigmatic, predicate=prag/sem, object=asset typed by syntax, graph≈pragmatics layer); (8) table_export pivot flattening → Arrow columnar mirrors with DuckDB/polars zero-copy; (9) DB-per-tenant → one datacrystal store per tenant (embedded model fits exactly); (10) blob_store with content-hash → blob entities or a blob sidecar; (11) dedup redirect/union resolution (sda_union recursive CTE) → should become a maintained index/materialized entity set updated on commit delta, not a per-read view — datacrystal's dirty-tracking + commit pipeline is the right place. DOMAIN/application semantics that should stay ON TOP as an 'sda' or 'semantic-outline' package: the semiotic vocabulary and layer conventions (outline, PBI, skos, owl, dcterms, identifiers, comment, prov, de.semanticworks.invoice), cardinality constraint policy, the published/head-document convention, inline-vs-uri children semantics, render processors (plain text/HTML outlines), SKOS synonym-expansion direction policy, dedup matching rules + canonical election, SWARM push contract/hierarchy building (inbox/sync batches), needs_review roll-ups, search ranking policy (published-first, two-pass terminology), and Business-Map concept extraction. STRUCTURAL TENSION to resolve: SDA is deliberately schema-free EAV (any producer adds any layer without migration) while datacrystal's core is typed slots-dataclasses; the clean synthesis is a generic `Fact`/`Statement` record type (msgspec) in datacrystal — or in the RDF extension — carrying the 5-tuple with bitmap-indexed dimension fields, with typed entities optionally projected per pragmatics layer; plus an asyncio ownership story (semantic-studio is fully async; ADR-001 owner-thread confinement maps to single-event-loop confinement). Verdict the evidence supports: SDA-the-store is ~70% reimplementable as datacrystal features (and would directly cure the measured pathologies: per-read recursive CTE, 5-column self-join latest resolution, full-index rebuilds, read-back idempotency); SDA-the-model (semiotics, outlines, SKOS, dedup policy) is application/domain vocabulary that should be a layer or extension package over datacrystal, not core — with the 5-tuple fact/statement primitive being the one SDA element worth promoting INTO the datacrystal concept (likely as/alongside the RDF extension).

judgment.verdict: SDA goes ON TOP of datacrystal — as a named application-layer package (the SDA model/vocabulary) plus app code in semantic-studio — with a principled, narrow back-flow of exactly four generic requirements into datacrystal (unique secondary-key lookup, frozen/append-only entity mode, earlier FTS sidecar, a future ledger extension on the watermark pipeline). It must NOT become part of datacrystal's core concept. The argument: every major SDA element fails at least one layering test for core inclusion — the semiotic axes, relation-layer vocabularies, dedup policy, authority shadowing, and GoBD compliance are domain knowledge the database cannot have (test 2) and that no non-SDA user wants (test 1); meanwhile everything SDA needs from storage is either already on the ratified roadmap (bitmap indexes + Condition AST, FTS5/usearch sidecars on the commit-delta/watermark pipeline, reverse-reference index, snapshots, Arrow mirrors, RDF extension) or is a one-flag generalization of it. The evidence from sda_store is decisive: all five measured slowness causes (per-read recursive-CTE dedup clustering, latest-version GROUP BY over full asset text, N+1 Python reconstruction, rebuild-the-world FTS indexing, per-row commits) are symptoms of hand-rolling persistence primitives in application SQL — i.e., SDA is the proof-by-counterexample for datacrystal's existence, not a missing core feature. Strategically, SDA's identity as a citable formal model (5-paper pipeline, SEMANTiCS window) is better served by 'SDA: a semiotic data model, reference implementation on datacrystal' than by dissolving into a database's feature list; and the solo-maintainer/24-month constraint ratified in ROADMAP.md cannot absorb a second conceptual universe in core. Datacrystal's typed slots-dataclass identity and SDA's schema-free EAV are philosophically opposed — the synthesis is typed entities for SDA's stable layers (outlines, concepts, blobs) and a generic frozen Statement entity for the schema-free long tail, both as ordinary datacrystal application classes.
judgment.layering_map: SDA 7-tuple record (sigmatics, pragmatics, semantics, syntax, asset, fingerprint, editstamp) -> SDA application layer (one @entity slots-dataclass, frozen) -> core object engine: msgspec records + OID registry. | sigmatics URI identity -> app field with unique constraint -> EntityMap unique identity index over OID registry (promote to explicit v0.x commitment). | hierarchical sigmatics / has_children+has_parents outline topology -> app entities with list[Lazy[Node]] -> Lazy[T] + v1 reverse-reference index + traversal API (kills core.py:845 substring scans and parent-chain N+1 walks). | pragmatics/semantics/syntax orthogonal axis queries -> axis VOCABULARY is app; FILTERING is core -> pyroaring bitmap indexes + Condition AST (roadmap v0.x item 4; literal replacement for all WHERE pragmatics='X' raw SQL). | asset payload -> app field -> msgspec body; large binaries as separate Blob entities (bytes + Lazy) on SQLite-blob storage; out-of-band buffers for numpy/Arrow. | fingerprint (SHA-256) -> app-computed field at emit (one line; distinct from core's per-record durability checksums) -> record field + bitmap/unique index. | editstamp + version history -> app field; temporal reads -> commit watermarks, store.snapshot(), session(before=watermark) (ADR-001 rider 2 / held-open historical sessions). | emit/revoke append-only invariant -> SPLIT: enforcement = small core feature (@entity(frozen=True): dirty tracking simply never re-arms); revoke-as-record + current-state projection = app logic -> tri-state dirty tracking + commit pipeline. | latest-wins 'current view' -> app-maintained typed projection entities updated in the same commit -> live entities + identity map (O(1) lookup replaces 5-column self-join GROUP BY). | authority shadowing (proposed 8th field) -> app projection policy (OUTLOOK.md itself says 'configuration, not model') -> bitmap index on authority field. | identifiers/owl/skos/dcterms relation layers -> app vocabulary; edges as typed references -> reverse-reference index (v1). | dedup (uid->sameAs clusters, canonical election, redirects, cluster TOC 'virtual SDAs') -> app service: incremental union-find at write time, DedupCluster/Redirect derived entities -> commit-delta/watermark pipeline consumer (replaces per-read sda_union recursive CTE). | SKOS concepts, terminology import, Business-Map extraction -> app entities + app code. | FTS + synonym expansion -> SPLIT: FTS5 sidecar = datacrystal[fts] extension (pyr.FullText); synonym direction policy + two-pass ranking = app -> watermark pipeline. | Triple Sigmatics dual embeddings -> datacrystal[vector] extension (DESIGN.md already specifies one .usearch file per @Vector field, so 2 vectors/entity is covered); model-version-tagged historical vectors = app entities. | LLM reasoning agent / 3-layer search -> pure app (semantic-studio). | RDF projection + SPARQL ('Approach C hybrid') -> datacrystal-rdf extension (roadmap punt #17 — SDA is its design customer and exact match: store primary, triples projected, rdflib facade). | GoBD Merkle audit chain + completeness proofs -> NEW extension candidate datacrystal[ledger], a Tier-3 CDC consumer of the public commit-delta contract (SCALING.md Tier 3 verbatim) -> watermark pipeline. | GoBD retention timers, legal hold, Z3/Beschreibungsstandard export, Verfahrensdokumentation, IDW PS 880 -> app (export rides v1 Arrow mirrors/DuckDB). | table_export/pivot -> app over Arrow columnar mirrors (v1). | multi-tenancy DB-per-tenant -> app TenantRegistry opening one store per org via aopen() -> multi-store per tenant (explicitly held open in ADR-001). | plugin SQL-view injection mechanism -> dies; replaced by app-maintained derived entities + Condition AST; the extension seam IS the watermark-consumer contract.
judgment.what_core_gains: SDA as first customer contributes: (1) PROMOTE unique secondary-key index (string alias -> entity) from implied EntityMap detail to an explicit v0.x commitment — sigmatic-URI lookup is SDA's hottest read path and idempotent upsert-by-uid is its write-path idempotency mechanism; generic for any app with natural keys (test 3: everyone rebuilds this badly). (2) ADD frozen/append-only entity mode (@entity(frozen=True) or append_only collection: update raises, delete forbidden outside GC policy) — near-zero cost given tri-state dirty tracking (entities never arm Dirty), and generic for the named agent-memory persona (episodic logs, transcripts, provenance) — test 1 and 3 both pass; this is the only SDA invariant worth enforcing in core, and it benefits everyone without constraining mutable entities (test 4 passes because it is opt-in per class). (3) RESEQUENCE datacrystal[fts] earlier — the ratified roadmap defers all extensions until 'after v1 core freeze', but roadmap item 3 calls the commit-delta/watermark pipeline 'the single most load-bearing undelivered component'; a public contract with zero consumers is untested. FTS5 is the cheapest consumer (stdlib) and SDA needs it; pull it back to late v0.x as the pipeline's validation harness (DESIGN.md's original v0.2 had exactly this). (4) ADD to the punt list: datacrystal[ledger] — hash-chained commit log + Merkle inclusion/completeness proofs as a Tier-3 watermark consumer; demand-driven, differentiating for audit and agent-provenance use cases; its only requirement on core is deterministic, stably-ordered, replayable commit deltas — already promised in item 3, so SDA's GoBD requirement becomes a free conformance test of that promise. (5) VALIDATE the asyncio story early: semantic-studio is fully async (FastAPI+NiceGUI), so aopen() loop binding, store.transaction(), and the 'critical section is the code between awaits' doctrine get a real customer in v0.x rather than at the v1 web extension. (6) ADD a batch-hydration API (load-many-by-OID) — the get_sdas_by_uris_batch lesson; trivial, generic, prevents N+1 from ever being the user's problem. (7) CONFIRM multi-vector commitment in docs (>=2 @Vector fields per entity for Triple Sigmatics). DEMOTIONS/non-gains: SDA does not justify accelerating Arrow mirrors (hundreds-to-thousands of rows per tenant; v1 timing fine), does not need the reverse-reference index before v1 (it already stores both edge directions explicitly), does not move the RDF extension off the punt list (Wave-3 SPARQL runs over in-memory RDFLib today and can keep doing so at app level), and adds zero pressure toward any 'Never' item.
judgment.what_to_avoid: (1) Schema-free EAV/statement storage as a core mode — it destroys datacrystal's identity (typed slots-dataclasses AS the schema), violates the hard 'never expose object-dtype columns' rule for Arrow mirrors, and degrades bitmap indexing to string-soup; if a generic Statement/quad primitive is ever wanted, it is the datacrystal-rdf extension's job, not core's. (2) Semiotic axis names (sigmatics/pragmatics/semantics/syntax) anywhere in core API or docs — SDA's own corpus can't keep pragmatism-vs-pragmatics straight (published erratum); a database must not carry a contested domain vocabulary. (3) GoBD/compliance semantics in core (retention periods, legal hold, WORM, Z3 export, IDW PS 880 certification posture) — regulatory domain knowledge with audit liability, fatal to a solo maintainer; test 2 failure in its purest form. (4) Merkle-root computation in the core commit path — taxes every commit of every user for a compliance feature; the watermark pipeline makes it a lossless sidecar, so in-core placement buys nothing (test 4 failure: constrains everyone, benefits only SDA). (5) Dedup/sameAs/canonical-election/redirect resolution in the core read path — this is the sda_union mistake (per-read recomputation of domain policy) recreated; core reads must stay O(lookup), projections are app-maintained derived data. Oldest-wins election and manual override are policy, not storage. (6) Authority-based conflict shadowing in core — it is LWW-with-authority application policy; the roadmap's 'Never' list already rejected richer-than-LWW conflict semantics when it rejected core CRDTs. (7) Multi-tenancy registry in core — multi-store-per-tenant is already the answer; a tenant concept in core duplicates the filesystem. (8) 'Current-state projection' / materialized-view DSL in core v0.x — tempting generalization of SDA's latest-wins need, but it is a query-engine commitment; the watermark-consumer contract is the principled seam, and app code owns projections until >=2 more customers demand otherwise. Each of these would also break the ratified scope discipline: core caps at two sidecar technologies, ~24-month v1.0, solo maintainer.
judgment.migration_sketch: Rebuild as 'sda' package over datacrystal (gated on core v0.x items 1-4 plus the FTS extension — /Users/sh/pyrsistance is design-docs-only today, so this is the first-customer workload, not a this-month port). ENTITIES (typed, for stable layers): OutlineNode(sigmatic: Annotated[str, pyr.Id-unique], title, description: Annotated[str, pyr.FullText], children: list[Lazy[OutlineNode]], parents: list[Lazy[OutlineNode]], published: datetime|None, pbi_status, needs_review); BoardElement; SkosConcept(label, definition, broader: list[Lazy[SkosConcept]], synonyms: list[str], exact_match: list[Lazy]); Blob(sha256 unique, mime, filename, data: Lazy bytes); DedupCluster(canonical: Lazy, members: list[Lazy], elected_at) + Redirect entities. SCHEMA-FREE TAIL: one frozen Statement entity (@entity(frozen=True)) carrying the 5-tuple (sigmatic, pragmatics, semantics, syntax, asset_json) + fingerprint + editstamp, with pyr.Index bitmap markers on the three categorical axes — emit = store(), revoke = a frozen revocation Statement; this preserves SDA's append-only event-log semantics and audit history verbatim while typed entities serve as the maintained 'latest' projection updated in the same commit. INDEX/SIDECAR MAPPING: sigmatic lookup -> unique identity index; all axis scans -> pyroaring bitmaps + Condition AST; search_index+FTS5 rebuild machinery -> deleted, replaced by datacrystal[fts] sidecar fed O(delta) by commit watermarks (breadcrumbs become a denormalized field maintained at write); Wave-4 embeddings -> datacrystal[vector], two @Vector fields; sda_union/v_clusters views -> deleted, dedup runs as incremental union-find at write time (commit-delta consumer) materializing DedupCluster/Redirect entities; table_export -> Arrow mirror + DuckDB when v1 lands; SPARQL Wave 3 -> keep in-memory RDFLib projection in-app now, adopt datacrystal-rdf extension if/when it ships; GoBD Merkle layer -> datacrystal[ledger] watermark consumer. TENANCY: TenantRegistry survives shape-identical, opening one store per org dir (data/{org}/studio.dc) via aopen() on the event loop; per-tenant asyncio.Lock replaced by ADR-001 confinement + store.transaction(). WHAT FIXES THE SLOWNESS — structurally fixed, not moved: per-read recursive CTE -> moved to write time and made incremental (the correct side; cost now proportional to new sameAs edges, not to corpus x reads); latest-version GROUP BY over asset text -> gone, 'current' is the live entity, O(1) identity-map/OID hit; N+1 + per-row json.loads/Pydantic -> gone, msgspec decode ~93ns/obj, identity-map caching, Lazy batch hydration, real references instead of string joins; full search rebuild per write batch -> O(delta) watermark application; one-commit-per-row imports -> buffer-until-commit storer with group-commit fsync. WHAT REMAINS/MOVES: write-time dedup maintenance is app code that must be written and tested (complexity relocates from opaque SQL views to debuggable Python — a win, but not free); Python object memory (~600B/obj) and 18-26ns/obj scan floors remain, irrelevant at SDA scale (10^2-10^5 rows/tenant vs the 1-5M envelope). STAYS IN SEMANTIC-STUDIO: FastAPI/NiceGUI UI, render processors, SWARM push contract (idempotency simplifies to upsert-by-uid), SKOS/TTL import, synonym-expansion direction policy, publish/unpublish workflow, dedup matching rules + election policy, retention/Z3/GoBD application layer, LLM search agent, Business-Map extraction. INTERIM (if pain bites before datacrystal v0.2-equivalent): materialize v_clusters into a table refreshed on dedup writes and add a latest-pointer table — two-day patches to sda_store that mirror the eventual architecture.
judgment.dissent: Strongest case for SDA-IN-CORE: the SDA 5-tuple IS the datom/quad primitive datacrystal already half-plans (the RDF extension), and the agent-memory persona — datacrystal's flagship — natively emits schema-free facts (LLM-extracted triples, observations, provenance). Fusing them would make datacrystal 'the semantic object database': a native Statement store with axis bitmaps, append-only semantics, fingerprints, and a Merkle-verifiable log as THE differentiator no embedded Python DB has, with SDA as the published formal model giving it academic legitimacy (Paper 1 becomes the database's theory paper). It loses on four grounds: (a) the ratified roadmap explicitly relegates triples to an extension (punt #17) and caps core at two sidecar technologies after a 14-agent study plus adversarial stress test — reversing that for the first customer is exactly the one-customer-driven scope creep the process exists to prevent; (b) schema-free EAV contradicts the load-bearing walls — typed dataclasses-as-schema, typed-column Arrow mirrors, the no-object-dtype rule — so fusion weakens both identities rather than strengthening either; (c) solo maintainer, 24-month honest v1.0: a second core data model roughly doubles format-freeze surface, migration surface, and docs; (d) sda_store's measured pathologies came precisely from entangling domain semantics (dedup views, latest-wins, virtual SDAs) with the storage read path — the lesson of the existing implementation is separation. SCENARIO WHERE IT WINS: if, post-v1, agent-memory demand converges on untyped fact emission as the dominant workload (multiple real customers asking for a statements API, not just SDA), then the SDA tuple should be ratified as the canonical statement format OF the datacrystal-rdf/statements extension — its spec, vocabulary-neutral, with frozen-entity enforcement and fingerprints — i.e., SDA-the-tuple graduates into the extension layer, still never into core.
